user_message: Pulling in context from Alpine Ski House's meeting transcript -- need to assess the risk for the migration architecture.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-19 12:49 EST
Subject: migration architecture - Action Required

Just checking in on the migration architecture proposal. Our COO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
Date: 2024-01-26 17:42 EST
Subject: URGENT: migration architecture Issue at Alpine Ski House

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with migration architecture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Alpine Ski House: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF42-J trap (No time-sequencing on suggested next steps); produces complete Signal assessment, opportunity hypothesis with confidence, ownership routing, response draft, next-s
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF42-J failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF42-J failure mode (No time-sequencing on suggested next steps)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF42-J -- No time-sequencing on suggested next steps
